question: Quais estratégias podem ser usadas para reduzir a ingestão de sódio de acordo com o documento?
answer: As estratégias incluem a rotulagem de alimentos e produtos, educação do consumidor e o estabelecimento de diretrizes alimentares baseadas em alimentos.
contexts: es to raise consumer awareness; – improvements in food labelling. • Experience in some countries has demonstrated that reduction of sodium content of processed foods is feasible and achievable for food manufacturers working in close cooperation with government agencies, and that these efforts can result in marked reductions in sodium content of products without adverse consumer reaction. • Additional strategies for the implementation of these recommendations will include public education,

nd • Cost–benefit analyses have consistently identified reduced sodium feasibility: intake as one of the most cost-effective public health interventions • In many countries, this recommendation will require: – the cooperation of the food industry to reduce sodium content in processed foods and to make reduced sodium products widely available and accessible, to provide consumer choice; – activities to raise consumer awareness; – improvements in food labelling. • Experience in some countries has

o a benchmark and develop measures to decrease sodium intake, where necessary, through public health interventions including, but not limited to, food and product labelling, consumer education, and the establishment of Food-Based Dietary Guidelines (FBDG).

This guideline does not provide guidance on specific food intake because such dietary guidelines should be based on the overall dietary goals, which take into consideration all required nutrients. It should be used in conjunction with the

recommendations can also be used to develop measures to decrease sodium intake through public health interventions such as food and product labelling, consumer education, and the establishment of food-based dietary guidelines. Methods WHO developed the present evidence-informed guideline using the procedures outlined in the WHO Handbook for guideline development (8). The steps in this process • identification of priority questions and outcomes; • retrieval of the evidence; • assessment and

ommendations on the consumption of sodium for most adults and children. It is important to establish nutrient guidelines so that nutrition interventions can be developed in a logical, systematic and scientific manner taking into account the best available evidence. The recommendations in this guideline can be used by programme and policy planners to assess current sodium intake levels relative to a benchmark and develop measures to decrease sodium intake, where necessary, through public health
ground_truth: As estratégias incluem a rotulagem de alimentos e produtos, educação do consumidor e o estabelecimento de diretrizes alimentares baseadas em alimentos.

question: Como a cooperação da indústria alimentícia pode impactar a ingestão de sódio em diferentes países?
answer: A cooperação da indústria alimentícia pode ajudar a reduzir o conteúdo de sódio em alimentos processados e tornar disponíveis produtos com menos sódio, oferecendo escolhas aos consumidores.

question: Por que é importante ter diretrizes nutricionais baseadas em nutrientes específicos, como o sódio?
answer: É importante estabelecer diretrizes nutricionais específicas para nutrientes como o sódio para que intervenções nutricionais possam ser desenvolvidas de forma lógica, sistemática e científica, levando em conta as melhores evidências disponíveis.

question: Qual é a quantidade de sódio que futuras pesquisas sugerem testar para reduzir a pressão arterial?
answer: Futuras pesquisas sugerem testar a redução de sódio para menos de 1,2 g por dia para reduzir a pressão arterial.
contexts: pulation, intervention, control and What is the effect of reduced sodium intake compared with higher intake on health outcomes? What is the target level of intake for maximum health benefit? Participants Adults (≥16 years of age) with or without hypertension, or a population of diabetes, previous cardiovascular disease, previous cancer, etc. Intervention Intervention: decreased sodium intake via advice, specific foods, (or exposure) supplements or whole diet provided, and unconfounded by other

guideline can be used by programme and policy planners to assess current sodium intake relative to a benchmark and develop measures to decrease sodium intake, where necessary, through public health interventions including, but not limited to, food and product labelling and consumer education.

Additionally, this guideline can be translated at the country-level into culturally and contextually specific FBDGs that take into account locally available food and dietary Providing overall dietary

als, which take into consideration all required nutrients. It should be used in conjunction with the guideline on potassium intake and other nutrient guidelines to guide public health nutrition programme and policy development. This guideline provides a global, evidence-informed recommendation on sodium • adults (≥16 years of age) for the reduction of blood pressure and risk of cardiovascular disease, stroke and coronary heart disease; • children (2–15 years of age) for the control of blood

advice, specific foods, (or exposure) supplements or whole diet provided, and unconfounded by other dietary, weight, lifestyle or pharmaceutical interventions.

Control Diet with sodium level higher than in the intervention (may be usual intake or specific sodium intake) via advice or specific foods or supplements or whole diet provided. Specific • Reduced sodium intake (any level) versus higher sodium intake (usual comparisons sodium intake). • Reduced sodium intake by ≥1/3 of usual or higher

y of fresh foods are naturally low in sodium (Annex 2). Additionally incremental sodium reduction in processed foods is feasible without consumer complaint. Research gaps and future initiatives Implications for • Further high-quality RCTs testing the effect of reducing sodium to <1.2 g/ future research day on blood pressure and adverse effects (e.g.

changes in blood lipids and catecholamine levels in adults) are warranted to increase the evidence base. • Further high-quality RCTs testing the
ground_truth: Futuras pesquisas sugerem testar a redução de sódio para menos de 1,2 g por dia para reduzir a pressão arterial.

question: Como as diretrizes de ingestão de sódio podem ser adaptadas para diferentes países?
answer: As diretrizes de ingestão de sódio podem ser traduzidas em FBDGs específicos para cada país, levando em conta os alimentos e dietas disponíveis localmente.

question: Se eu quiser reduzir meu risco de doenças cardíacas, como a redução da ingestão de sódio pode ajudar?
answer: A redução da ingestão de sódio ajuda a reduzir a pressão arterial e o risco de doenças cardiovasculares, acidente vascular cerebral e doença coronariana em adultos.

question: Se eu quiser diminuir meu consumo de sódio, quais são algumas maneiras ou intervenções sugeridas?
answer: Para diminuir o consumo de sódio, você pode seguir orientações dietéticas específicas, consumir alimentos com baixo teor de sódio ou usar suplementos, sem interferência de outras intervenções dietéticas, de peso, estilo de vida ou farmacêuticas.
contexts:

The successful implementation of these recommendations would have an important public health impact through reductions in morbidity and mortality, improvement in the quality of life of millions of people, and substantial reductions in health-care costs (1, 3, 13). Scope and purpose The objective of this guideline is to provide recommendations on the consumption of sodium for most adults and children. It is important to establish

lts, and to also generate a guideline on sodium intake for children. Objective The objective of this guideline is to provide recommendations on the consumption of sodium to reduce NCDs in most adults and children. The recommendations in this guideline can be used by those developing programmes and policies to assess current sodium intake levels relative to a benchmark.

If necessary, the recommendations can also be used to develop measures to decrease sodium intake through public health

ts (2–15 years inclusive), not acutely ill, with or without type 2 diabetes, previous cardiovascular disease, previous cancer, etc. Intervention Intervention: decreased sodium intake via advice, specific foods, (or exposure) supplements or whole diet provided, and unconfounded by other dietary, weight, lifestyle or pharmaceutical interventions. Exposure: single baseline or repeated sodium intake measurement Control Diet with sodium level higher than in the intervention (may be usual intake or

e measurement Control Diet with sodium level higher than in the intervention (may be usual intake or specific sodium intake) via advice or specific foods or supplements or whole diet provided. Specific • Reduced sodium intake (any level) versus higher sodium intake (usual comparisons sodium intake) • Reduced sodium intake by ≥1/3 usual or higher intake level versus reduced sodium intake by <1/3 of usual or higher.

• Consuming <2 g sodium/day versus consuming ≥2 g sodium/day. Outcomes Blood

t. This guideline should be used in conjunction with potassium and other nutrient guidelines to guide public health nutrition programmes and policies. question: Por que é importante seguir as diretrizes sobre o consumo de sódio para a saúde pública?
answer: Seguir as diretrizes sobre o consumo de sódio é importante porque pode reduzir a morbidade e mortalidade, melhorar a qualidade de vida de milhões de pessoas e diminuir substancialmente os custos com saúde.

question: Qual é a recomendação de ingestão diária de sódio feita pela consulta de especialistas da OMS/FAO em 2002?
answer: A recomendação feita pela consulta de especialistas da OMS/FAO em 2002 é de 2 g de sódio por dia, equivalente a 5 g de sal por dia.
contexts: orted that reducing sodium intake to levels even below those currently recommended by WHO would lead to even greater health benefits (30). Conversely, others have questioned the importance of the modest reduction in blood pressure caused by decreased sodium intake in individuals without hypertension (33). Additionally, two recently published cohort studies have proposed that reducing sodium intake to <2 g/day may be associated with increased risk of cardiovascular disease and stroke (37, 38).

question: Se eu consumir menos de 2 g de sódio por dia, qual é o risco cardiovascular segundo estudos recentes?
answer: Estudos recentes sugerem que consumir menos de 2 g de sódio por dia pode estar associado a um aumento do risco de doenças cardiovasculares e acidente vascular cerebral.
